preamble: The shift from [e] to [h] is typically a result of extreme devoicing or the development of aspiration (rough breathing) before initial vowels. It is historically prominent in the transition from various ancient Greek dialects.

examples.note: Development of 'rough breathing' (spiritus asper) adding an initial glottal fricative.
In rapid speech or whispering, unstressed vowels can devoice, leaving a glottal friction trace.
